raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section opens with a strong, potentially inflated contribution claim ('first ML method… clinically relevant accuracy'); reviewing requires checking whether the gap statement and framing are defensible given the evidence presented.","R1.2":"The inferential chain from narrow held-out benchmark numbers (MAE on three target classes) to the sweeping conclusion ('broad applicability in the pharmaceutical industry') needs coherence checking—the premises do not obviously support the scope of the conclusion.","R1.3":"Multiple specific claims ('12% reduction vs. DrugDock-v1', '28% reduction vs. chemistry-only baseline', 'consistent across classes') must be traceable to the reported figures; the self-constructed chemistry-only baseline materially weakens the 28% figure as an unbiased comparison point.","R1.7":"Both cited prior works appear as 'Anonymous Authors et al.', which likely indicates anonymized self-citation in a blinded submission—a scholarly integrity concern requiring flagging regardless of review type."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review focus on scientific and evidentiary concerns (claim validity, benchmark fairness, novelty relative to prior art), or on writing and presentation quality (prose clarity, paragraph structure, narrative flow)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section opens with a strong, potentially inflated contribution claim ('first ML method… clinically relevant accuracy'); reviewing requires checking whether the gap statement and framing are defensible given the evidence presented.
rationale.R1.2: The inferential chain from narrow held-out benchmark numbers (MAE on three target classes) to the sweeping conclusion ('broad applicability in the pharmaceutical industry') needs coherence checking—the premises do not obviously support the scope of the conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple specific claims ('12% reduction vs. DrugDock-v1', '28% reduction vs. chemistry-only baseline', 'consistent across classes') must be traceable to the reported figures; the self-constructed chemistry-only baseline materially weakens the 28% figure as an unbiased comparison point.
rationale.R1.7: Both cited prior works appear as 'Anonymous Authors et al.', which likely indicates anonymized self-citation in a blinded submission—a scholarly integrity concern requiring flagging regardless of review type.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus on scientific and evidentiary concerns (claim validity, benchmark fairness, novelty relative to prior art), or on writing and presentation quality (prose clarity, paragraph structure, narrative flow)?
